code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Predicates that describe a relationship where the object is the primary entity
    relational_predicates = {
        "leader", "capital", "currency", "anthem", "parentCompany", "location",
        "governingBody", "leaderTitle", "country", "isPartOf", "derivative",
        "recordLabel", "bandMember", "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"
    }

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language templates.
    # The placeholder {s} is subject, {o} is object (or combined objects).
    predicate_templates = {
        "leader": "{o} leads {s}",
        "leaderTitle": "{o} holds the title of {s}",
        "capital": "{o} is the capital of {s}",
        "currency": "{o} is the currency of {s}",
        "anthem": "{o} is the anthem of {s}",
        "parentCompany": "{o} is the parent company of {s}",
        "location": "{o} is located in {s}",
        "governingBody": "{o} governs {s}",
        "country": "{o} is part of {s}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "derivative": "{o} is a derivative of {s}",
        "recordLabel": "{s} is signed to {o}",
        "bandMember": "{o} is a member of {s}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": None  # handled specially
    }

    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        # Relationship predicates where object is the primary entity
        if p in relational_predicates:
            if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
                # Handle one or multiple associated bands
                bands = [b.replace("(band)", "").strip() for b in o.split(", ")]
                # If there are multiple bands, use "both" or list with commas
                if len(bands) > 2:
                    band_str = ", ".join(bands[:-1]) + ", and " + bands[-1]
                elif len(bands) == 2:
                    band_str = " and ".join(bands)
                else:
                    band_str = bands[0]
                verb = "played with" if "both" not in band_str else "played with both"
                # Simplify phrasing
                return f"{s} {verb} {band_str}"
            # Use predefined template if available
            tmpl = predicate_templates.get(p)
            if tmpl:
                return tmpl.format(s=s, o=o)
            # Fallback generic phrasing
            return f"{o} is the {p} of {s}"
        # Birth / death information
        if p in ("birthDate", "birthYear"):
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p in ("deathDate", "deathPlace"):
            return f"{s} died in {o}"
        if p == "birthPlace":
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p == "origin":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        # Genre – natural phrasing
        if p == "genre":
            # e.g., "The musical genre of Aaron Deer is indie rock."
            return f"The musical genre of {s} is {o.lower()}"
        # Instrument – handle both artist and genre subjects
        if p == "instrument":
            # If the subject looks like a genre (contains the word "music"), describe the instrument's association
            if "music" in s.lower():
                # e.g., "The Banjo is an instrument used in Country music."
                return f"The {o} is an instrument used in {s}"
            else:
                # Assume subject is a person/artist
                return f"{s} plays the {o}"
        # Occupation
        if p == "occupation":
            return f"{s} works as {o}"
        # Nationality
        if p == "nationality":
            return f"{s} is {o}"
        # Associated band / musical artist – phrase as collaboration
        if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            band_name = o.replace("(band)", "").strip()
            # Use a natural verb depending on context
            return f"{s} played with the band {band_name}"
        # Record label
        if p == "recordLabel":
            return f"{s} is signed to {o}"
        # Background
        if p == "background":
            return f"{s} has a background as {o}"
        # Active years
        if p == "activeYearsStartYear":
            return f"{s} started their career in {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsEndYear":
            return f"{s} ended their career in {o}"
        # Fallback
        return f"{s} {p} {o}"

    # Combine objects that share the same subject and predicate
    from collections import defaultdict

    grouped: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        grouped[(t.subject, t.predicate)].append(t.object)

    combined_triples: list[Triple] = []
    for (s, p), objs in grouped.items():
        if len(objs) == 1:
            o = objs[0]
        else:
            # join multiple objects with commas and "and"
            o = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]
        combined_triples.append(Triple(s, p, o))

    # Generate a clause for each (possibly combined) triple
    clauses = [format_triple(t) for t in combined_triples]

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

    }

    def format_triple(t: Triple) -> str:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
        # Relationship predicates where object is the primary entity
        if p in relational_predicates:
            return f"{o} is the {p} of {s}"
        # Birth / death information
        if p in ("birthDate", "birthYear"):

        if p in ("deathDate", "deathPlace"):
            return f"{s} died in {o}"
        if p == "birthPlace":
            return f"{s} was born in {o}"
        if p == "origin":
            return f"{s} originates from {o}"
        # Genre – natural phrasing
        if p == "genre":
            # e.g., "The musical genre of Aaron Deer is indie rock."
            return f"The musical genre of {s} is {o.lower()}"
        # Instrument – handle both artist and genre subjects
        if p == "instrument":
            # If the subject looks like a genre (contains the word "music"), describe the instrument's association
            if "music" in s.lower():
                # e.g., "The Banjo is an instrument used in Country music."
                return f"The {o} is an instrument used in {s}"
            else:
                # Assume subject is a person/artist
                return f"{s} plays the {o}"
        # Occupation
        if p == "occupation":
            return f"{s} works as {o}"
        # Nationality
        if p == "nationality":
            return f"{s} is {o}"
        # Associated band / musical artist – phrase as collaboration
        if p == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
            band_name = o.replace("(band)", "").strip()
            # Use a natural verb depending on context
            return f"{s} played with the band {band_name}"
        # Record label
        if p == "recordLabel":
            return f"{s} is signed to {o}"
        # Background
        if p == "background":
            return f"{s} has a background as {o}"
        # Active years
        if p == "activeYearsStartYear":
            return f"{s} started their career in {o}"
        if p == "activeYearsEndYear":
            return f"{s} ended their career in {o}"
        # Fallback
        return f"{s} {p} {o}"

    # Combine objects that share the same subject and predicate
    from collections import defaultdict

    grouped: dict[tuple[str, str], list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        grouped[(t.subject, t.predicate)].append(t.object)

    combined_triples: list[Triple] = []
    for (s, p), objs in grouped.items():
        if len(objs) == 1:
            o = objs[0]
        else:
            # join multiple objects with commas and "and"
            o = ", ".join(objs[:-1]) + " and " + objs[-1]
        combined_triples.append(Triple(s, p, o))

    # Generate a clause for each (possibly combined) triple
    clauses = [format_triple(t) for t in combined_triples]

    # Combine clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence
